LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - THE COURT ORDERS THAT A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHALL ISSUE. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE BALANCE OF HARDSHIP TILTS ON THE SIDE OF THE PLAINTIFF. IF A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS NOT ISSUED, PLAINTIFF WILL LOSE THE PROPERTY. LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 24, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER EX PARTE ORDER OF MAY 15, 2013. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, AFTER MEETING AND CONFERRING PER THE COURT'S INSTRUCTION, PARTIES HAVE REACHED AN AGREEMENT AND DEFENDANT ALLEN JOHNSON'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS WAS WITHDRAWN BY THE MOVING PARTY. NO FURTHER HEARING WAS NECESSARY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens
Off Calendar May-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Arthur Meirson, Esq. and R. Stephen Goldstein, Esq. of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad Harris & McSparran, Tel. 415.673.5600, for defendant and moving party;
Summer Smith, Esq. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, Tel. 650.364.8200, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: After meeting and conferring per the Court's instruction, parties have reached an agreement and DEFENDANT ALLEN JOHNSON'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens was withdrawn by the moving party. No further hearing was necessary. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Oppos Due:5/16; Reply Due:5/17)
Off Calendar May-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Summer Smith, Esq. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, Tel. 650.364.8200, for plaintiff and moving party;
Arthur Meirson, Esq. and R. Stephen Goldstein, Esq. of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad Harris & McSparran, Tel. 415.673.5600, for defendant and moving party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court orders that a Preliminary Injunction shall issue. The Court finds that the balance of hardship tilts on the side of the plaintiff. If a Preliminary Injunction is not issued, plaintiff will lose the property.
(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Oppos Due:5/16; Reply Due:5/17)
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Continued To May-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is Continued to May 24, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 per ex parte order of May 15, 2013. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
